# Chart

Composes Unovis charts with StackTrace colors, tooltips, and legends.

## Installation

Add Chart from the configured StackTrace registry. The frontend package uses the installed `@unovis/vue` dependency for chart marks, axes, containers, crosshairs, and tooltips.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/chart
```

## Usage

Define plain application data and a typed `ChartConfig`, then compose the appropriate Unovis primitives inside `ChartContainer`. StackTrace does not wrap every chart type, so Unovis remains the source of truth for mark and axis props.

```vue
<template>
  <ChartContainer :config="chartConfig" class="min-h-64 w-full max-w-xl">
    <VisXYContainer :data="chartData">
      <VisGroupedBar
        :x="(datum: ChartDatum) => datum.month"
        :y="[(datum: ChartDatum) => datum.desktop, (datum: ChartDatum) => datum.mobile]"
        :color="[chartConfig.desktop.color, chartConfig.mobile.color]"
        :rounded-corners="4"
      />
    </VisXYContainer>
  </ChartContainer>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { VisGroupedBar, VisXYContainer } from '@unovis/vue'
import { ChartContainer, type ChartConfig } from '@/Components/Chart'

const chartData = [
  { month: 'Jan', desktop: 186, mobile: 80 },
  { month: 'Feb', desktop: 305, mobile: 200 },
  { month: 'Mar', desktop: 237, mobile: 120 },
  { month: 'Apr', desktop: 173, mobile: 190 },
  { month: 'May', desktop: 209, mobile: 130 },
  { month: 'Jun', desktop: 214, mobile: 140 },
]

type ChartDatum = typeof chartData[number]

const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: 'Desktop',
    color: 'var(--chart-1)',
  },
  mobile: {
    label: 'Mobile',
    color: 'var(--chart-2)',
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
</script>
```

## Configuration and theming

Each config key represents one presented series. Give it a readable `label` and either a single `color` or a complete light/dark `theme` map. `ChartContainer` publishes those values as `--color-<key>` custom properties and supplies the config to descendant chart helpers.

The required `config` prop describes presentation only. Keep raw records, aggregation, date normalization, units, and business rules outside the component. This keeps the same dataset usable in tables, exports, and accessible summaries.

## Tooltips and legends

The package re-exports Unovis `ChartTooltip` and `ChartCrosshair`. Use `componentToString` with `ChartTooltipContent` when a crosshair needs StackTrace-styled HTML. The tooltip content supports label formatting, dot, line, and dashed indicators, and optional label or indicator hiding.

`ChartLegendContent` reads labels, icons, and colors from the nearest container config. Place legends close to the visualization and avoid relying on color alone to distinguish series.

## Responsive layout

Chart Container fills its parent. Give it an explicit minimum height and let the surrounding grid control width. At small breakpoints, reduce the number of ticks or move the legend rather than shrinking labels until they are unreadable.

## Accessibility

A visual chart is not a complete accessible representation. Add a nearby heading and summary, expose units and time ranges in text, and provide a semantic table or equivalent detail when exact values matter. Tooltips are supplemental and must not be the only way to obtain data.

## When to use

- Use a chart to reveal trend, distribution, or comparison.
- Use Table when readers primarily need exact values or row-by-row lookup.
- Avoid chart decoration that does not help answer a concrete question.

Hosted documentation: [Chart](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/chart)
